_doc: Per-bucket data-quality tier system. Pinned by meta_version.json -> bucket_quality_hash. Buckets at tier `EXCLUDE_QUANT` are dropped entirely from cross-bucket lexical metrics (pairwise_similarity, isolation, bucket_exclusive_vocab) because their corpus is dominated by stub-only feeds (Reuters via Google News, China Global Times, etc.) where the tokenizer sees titles, not bodies — title statistics are not commensurable with full-body statistics. Tier `C` buckets ship with a confidence flag the renderer surfaces; they remain in metrics but the analyst is warned. Tier `A`/`B` is the default for healthy buckets. Adding a bucket here is a minor bump; changing a tier label or removing an entry is a major bump.

tier_definitions.A: Healthy: extraction success >70%, stub ratio <20%, multiple feeds.
tier_definitions.B: Acceptable: extraction success 50-70% OR single-feed bucket. Included in all metrics; renderer may flag.
tier_definitions.C: Narrative only: extraction success 30-50%. Included in metrics with confidence flag; not for cross-bucket comparative claims.
tier_definitions.EXCLUDE_QUANT: Quarantined from quantitative metrics. Stub-only aggregators where signal_text is the title, not the body. Bucket still appears in briefings for narrative coverage; it just doesn't contribute to Jaccard/cosine/exclusive-vocab numbers.

google_news_reuters.reason: 41/50 stubs in 2026-05-06 baseline; aggregator returns headlines+links, no body extractable

china_global_times.reason: 50/50 stubs; CGT RSS does not include article bodies

africa.reason: 6 feeds for 1.4B people across 50+ countries; structural underrepresentation flagged in docs/COVERAGE.md. Narrative coverage only; do not derive cross-bucket comparative claims.
